name: Cavitation Technologies, Inc.
business: Cavitation Technologies, Inc. develops and commercializes environmentally friendly technology-based systems for processing industrial liquids, including vegetable oil refining and water treatment. Its core patented Nano Reactor and Low Pressure Nano Reactor (LPN) are used in systems for vegetable oil refining, water treatment, and alcoholic beverage enhancement. Revenue is generated from reactor sales and licensing fees.
sellsTo: Produced water and frac water treatment in oil & gas

exposures.sentence: Our newly designed Low Pressure Nano Reactor ( LPN ) was specifically developed to be integrated into produced water treatment system along with our proprietary chemical formulations, and has depicted measurable and quantifiable advantages over industry standard processes and equipment.

In 2024, we installed our first system at Hacienda Farms (B&F Greenhouse Services, Inc.) in Canada. The system is currently undergoing trials to increase oxygen levels in the water, eliminate algae, and control bacterial growth, all without the use of harsh chemicals.

We have agreed to provide certain licensing rights related to our miniature low pressure nano-reactor (MLPN) to be used in developing and manufacturing of small home appliances to enhance alcoholic beverages.

Along with improving our existing technologies, we have developed Hydroplasma, an innovative process combining cavitation and cold plasma technology to enhance our water treatment efficiency, which: Breaks down both organic and inorganic compounds.

catalysts.event: Sales expected for water treatment in first half of fiscal 2026
catalysts.sentence: The Company anticipates that sales will be generated in the first half of fiscal 2026.

catalysts.event: Completion of trials at Hacienda Farms by first calendar quarter of 2026
catalysts.sentence: The outcome of the trials mentioned above, which we expect to be completed within the first calendar quarter of 2026, will determine the commercial viability of our product and the timeline for any potential revenue generation.

catalysts.event: Earliest sales and revenue for Barmuze anticipated in second half of 2026
catalysts.sentence: The earliest sales and revenue for Barmuze are anticipated in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Sales and revenue for Hydro-Plasma anticipated in first half of fiscal 2026 if trials successful
catalysts.sentence: Upon completion of multiple trials, if successful, sales and revenue are anticipated in the first half of fiscal 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global cold plasma market projected to grow from $1.5 billion in 2021 to $3.1 billion by 2027
tamClaims.sentence: The global cold plasma market is projected to grow from $1.5 billion in 2021 to $3.1 billion by 2027, fueled by increasing demand for sustainable water solutions.

tamClaims.claim: Water treatment market in Canada valued at approximately $2.51 billion
tamClaims.sentence: The overall market for water treatment in Canada is valued at approximately $2.51 billion, with continuous expansion due to the demand for sustainable solutions in agriculture and industrial applications.
